summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including approval of a 4th of July parade organized by the Goshen Chamber of Commerce, three zoning enforcement resolutions authorizing the Clermont County prosecutor to pursue violations under House Bill 315, a rezoning of parcels from M1 light industrial to B1 local business district at the former Combs cleanup site, and discussion of the Eagle's Nest development's connector road being designated as private. The board also approved police training requests, accepted a firefighter resignation, and discussed engaging Bricker legal counsel for TIF-related work on the Eagle's Nest/State Route 28 corridor project.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (Jan 28 - Feb 10, 2026) passed unanimously
Motion to approve 4th of July parade by Goshen Chamber of Commerce passed unanimously
Resolution R-003-2026 adopted authorizing prosecutor to pursue zoning enforcement at 7194 Goshen Road passed unanimously
Resolution R-004-2026 adopted authorizing prosecutor to pursue zoning enforcement at 1629 Woodville Pike passed unanimously
Resolution R-005-2026 adopted authorizing prosecutor to pursue zoning enforcement at 7000 Willow Ridge passed unanimously
Resolution to initiate rezoning of parcels from M1 to B1 at Combs cleanup site passed unanimously
Motion to approve Officer Maggie Weiderholt's crime scene reconstruction training ($725) passed unanimously
Motion to approve SRO training conference in Sandusky ($830 per officer) passed unanimously
Motion to increase fire department appropriations by $11,500 for Tender 18 repairs passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter/paramedic Steven Fisher effective Jan 29, 2026 passed unanimously
Motion to enter agreement with Charles E. Harris and Associates for Hinkle report preparation passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for administrative assistant Jen to symposium in Erlanger, KY ($75 registration) passed unanimously
Motion to appoint Trustee Hauserman as voting representative to Clermont County General Health District Advisory Council on March 19 passed (Hauserman abstained)
Motion to approve Eagle's Nest development connector road as a private road passed unanimously
Motion to accept $50 Sonic Drive-In gift card donation passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 6:44 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Administrator Rose to coordinate operations plan for 4th of July parade with Chamber of Commerce, police, and CPAAA
Director Langenheim to work on adopting international property maintenance code provisions for enforcement of private road upkeep
Administrator Rose to arrange meeting with Bricker legal counsel regarding Eagle's Nest TIF and bring proposal to next meeting
Director Langenheim to continue preparing land use plan with public session scheduled for March 3 with Drew DeMarche from county planning
Dan to get quotes for iPads for the board
Lisa/Donna to survey CPAAA volunteers for 4th of July parade staffing availability
Bob Allen to distribute gift cards donated by Steve (zoning board member) to staff at a future meeting
key_amounts: $725 registration for Officer Weiderholt crime scene reconstruction training
$830 per officer for SRO training conference in Sandusky
$11,500 increased appropriation for Tender 18 fire apparatus repairs
$650-$850 estimated cost for Charles E. Harris and Associates to prepare Hinkle report
$75 registration fee for administrative assistant symposium in Erlanger, KY
$50 Sonic Drive-In gift card donation accepted
notable_topics: 4th of July parade approval for America's 250th celebration
Three zoning enforcement resolutions under House Bill 315 for nuisance properties
Rezoning of Combs cleanup site parcels from M1 industrial to B1 business district
Eagle's Nest development connector road designated as private road
Eagle's Nest TIF discussion and potential engagement of Bricker legal counsel
State Route 28 corridor widening from two to five lanes
New medic unit delivery moved up to last quarter of 2027
Land use plan public session scheduled for March 3
Snow removal and winter storm response commendation
Police officer training and SRO conference approvals
Director Langenheim completed master's degree in public administration from EKU
attendees: Laura (fiscal officer Lisa England or staff)
Steve (former Fire Chief, Zoning Board member, 6336 Telford Farm Lane)
Aaron Ellermann (GCC family fireworks organizer, mentioned)
Goshen Chamber of Commerce representative (parade presenter)
notes: The meeting referenced Trustee Kugel, Trustee Grimaldi, and Trustee Snell during one roll call, which appear to be transcript errors. The 4th of July parade is planned to coincide with America's 250th birthday celebration and Goshen Community Church's 5th annual event. The Eagle's Nest development discussion revealed coordinated planning between the developer, ODOT, County Engineer, and Goshen Township for major State Route 28 corridor improvements. The board expressed interest in engaging Bricker legal counsel for TIF negotiations but deferred a formal motion to a future meeting. Steve, a zoning board member and former fire chief, donated his zoning stipend as gift cards for township staff.
title: BOT 2-10-26
